visions.wristbands: none in the vision; the cuffs have not yet been given, and Keen is checking his satchel beside his Mum before departure
visions.style: Aida’s POV into the vision: the vision bubble fills the entire frame, with only a soft rose-quartz haze and orb-edge at the outermost margins dissolving into darkness and mist; no sanctuary or Aida visible around it.
visions.materialize: Rose-quartz light coalesces within the full frame out of soft darkness, resolving into the later pier goodbye image before fading gently back to Aida.

recurring.name: Squeaky
recurring.appearance: Canonical Squeaky dolphin model sheet; playful dolphin chirps and clicks; no added markings or accessories.
recurring.orientation: Introduced frame-right of Keen’s boat in Scene 3, then keeps pace beside the boat through Scene 4; rescue orientation follows Keen toward the glowing shore.

recurring.name: Keen's Mum
recurring.appearance: Canonical Keen’s Mum reference; adult female sized to Aida class, no clothes, gold scalloped collar and light-blue bow only.
recurring.orientation: Anchored on the pier facing seaward as Keen departs; remains the emotional home point behind him.

recurring.name: Fuzzby and Zenny pair lock
recurring.appearance: Fuzzby is the bigger male bee and Zenny is the smaller female bee; keep Fuzzby frame-left and Zenny frame-right whenever they share a frame.
recurring.orientation: Fuzzby’s motion is fast, chaotic and forward-driving; Zenny holds steadier, cleaner counter-positioning.

persistent.in: On Keen’s wrists from the gift moment in Scene 3 through Scenes 4, 5 if glimpsed at sea, 6 if seen through rain, 7 underwater, and 8 on the beach
On Keen’s wrists from the Aquamarine crystal settling in Scene 9 through Scene 10 and onward
Crystal Cove shore scenes after the storm: wet sand, driftwood and grasses persist from Scene 6 into Scenes 8 and 10

lost.name: Keen’s map
lost.reason: The storm wind snatches it from Keen’s paws and it vanishes into the rain, making his uncertainty physical and irreversible.

lost.name: Keen’s sailboat
lost.reason: During the rescue and wave crash, the boat is lost in the storm; Misty confirms it is gone as Keen returns on Squeaky.

items.name: Crystal bowl
items.appearance: Sanctuary sound bowl with wand; source of Aida’s low shimmering tone and vision entry.

items.name: Keen’s satchel
items.appearance: Travel satchel checked repeatedly by Keen before departure; treated as an environment-carried prop, never attached as a new body feature.

items.name: Rolled map
items.appearance: Simple travel map handled by Keen at sea; must be readable as a loose paper prop that can be wind-snapped away.

items.name: Berry pouch
items.appearance: Small packed supply pouch loaded into the sailboat with other departure items.

items.name: Blanket
items.appearance: Rolled travel blanket loaded into the sailboat as part of Keen’s careful preparation.

items.name: Small sailboat
items.appearance: Neat little sailboat with mast, sail, rope tie, and room for Keen’s supplies; starts at the pier, then rides open water, then is lost in the storm.

items.name: Father’s gold cuffs, vacant
items.appearance: Gold cuffs with empty kite-shaped recesses; no gems present until Scene 9; faint self-glow underwater in Scene 7 comes from within, not from installed crystals.

items.name: Father’s gold cuffs, crystal-set
items.appearance: Gold cuffs with faceted Aquamarine kite gems settled into the recesses from Scene 9 onward.

items.name: Ragged drift net
items.appearance: Torn, rough net wrapped around Squeaky’s tail underwater; it is an obstacle from the storm environment, not a villain.

items.name: Aquamarine crystal
items.appearance: Glowing aquamarine light that forms between Aida and Keen, splits into two smaller gems, and settles into the cuffs as recognition.

worldState.change: Warm playful flower field receives the first distant thunder and slight light shift.
The sanctuary quiets for Aida’s bowl tone, then distant wind enters as the vision ends.
Keen leaves the dock; his Mum remains behind as the emotional home anchor, and the boat departs into open water.
Clear open water becomes storm-closed, with map lost and horizon erased.
Forest edge becomes a lookout into danger; Fuzzby and Zenny redirect from returning home to raising the alarm.
Cove shifts from storm-battered uncertainty to active beacon site; wet sand, shells, driftwood and wind-bent grasses establish positions for later return.
Underwater net is torn; Squeaky is freed; Keen’s empty cuffs glow faint aquamarine for the first time; the sailboat is lost.
Storm breaks and the shore becomes a receiving place; Squeaky departs to carry word home.
Post-storm gathering circle becomes Keen’s welcome place; Aquamarine gems settle into the cuffs.
Beach settles into golden-hour peace; wet leaves and sand retain the storm’s trace while the group walks into everyday belonging.
